signalTimeline.title: ECDC surveillance update — 11 cases, 3 deaths
signalTimeline.description: ECDC publishes an updated surveillance summary: 9 confirmed and 2 probable cases (11 total) with 3 deaths reported across 9 countries; risk for the EU/EEA assessed as VERY LOW.

signalTimeline.title: ECDC reports 9 confirmed, 2 probable cases
signalTimeline.description: ECDC updates its Andes hantavirus outbreak surveillance page to 9 confirmed cases, 2 probable cases, 0 suspected cases, and 3 deaths. ECDC notes that additional cases may still be identified because of the long incubation period, while the EU/EEA general-population risk remains very low.

signalTimeline.title: CDC orders quarantine for two UNMC passengers
signalTimeline.description: CDC issues federal quarantine orders for two MV Hondius passengers at the University of Nebraska Medical Center. AP reports 18 people from the ship are being assessed at UNMC, with 16 others asked to remain at the facility through May 31.

signalTimeline.title: WHO publishes rapid risk assessment v2
signalTimeline.description: WHO publishes version 2 of its global rapid risk assessment for the MV Hondius Andes hantavirus outbreak, addressing public health impact, geographic spread risk, and response-capacity considerations.

signalTimeline.title: PHAC confirms Canadian Andes hantavirus case
signalTimeline.description: PHAC confirms by National Microbiology Laboratory testing that one Canadian MV Hondius passenger is positive for Andes hantavirus. A travelling partner tested negative, no further Canadian cases had been identified at that time, and high-risk contacts remained in isolation and monitoring.

signalTimeline.title: Canada confirms 12th case — British Columbia; 9th country affected
signalTimeline.description: British Columbia confirms an Andes hantavirus case in a Canadian MV Hondius passenger — the 12th overall case and 9th country. BC Centre for Disease Control (BCCDC) and Public Health Agency of Canada (PHAC) coordinating response. No community transmission identified. Separately, a Colorado man dies of Sin Nombre hantavirus — an unrelated endemic exposure; Sin Nombre does NOT transmit person-to-person and has NO link to the cruise ship cluster.

signalTimeline.title: Emory releases both monitored passengers; U.S. confirms no cases
signalTimeline.description: Emory University Hospital releases both U.S. repatriation passengers after negative hantavirus testing. Combined with the UNMC patient's retesting (negative), CDC confirms no U.S. cases from MV Hondius. CDC also updates home monitoring guidance for the approximately 41 Americans still in the 42-day observation window across 11 states.

signalTimeline.title: Ebola readiness signal added for DRC and neighbouring-region monitoring
signalTimeline.description: Monitoring record added for Ebola disease preparedness and source verification; publish case counts or health-zone details only from a specific WHO or Africa CDC outbreak report.

signalTimeline.title: WHO emergency scientific consultation on Andes virus countermeasures
signalTimeline.description: WHO convenes emergency scientific consultation on Andes hantavirus medical countermeasures — reviewing favipiravir, investigational monoclonal antibodies, and DNA/mRNA vaccine candidates including the USAMRIID phase-1 DNA vaccine. WHO DG Tedros holds press conference reaffirming global risk as LOW; no community transmission identified. U.S. inconclusive case (UNMC) confirmed negative after retesting — U.S. removed from active case roster.

signalTimeline.title: U.S. measles surveillance remains under review
signalTimeline.description: Monitoring record added for U.S. measles outbreak surveillance and Americas elimination-status implications; verify current counts against CDC and PAHO updates.

signalTimeline.title: Mpox clade I and importation readiness remains under review
signalTimeline.description: Monitoring record added for mpox clinical and laboratory readiness; verify current country trends and importation details against specific Africa CDC, WHO, or CDC updates.

signalTimeline.title: Zoonotic influenza watch remains active
signalTimeline.description: Monitoring record added for H5 animal-health activity and sporadic human-exposure investigations; verify subtype-specific human case details against WHO or CDC event updates.

signalTimeline.title: Cholera preparedness signal added for sub-Saharan Africa
signalTimeline.description: Monitoring record added for cholera preparedness where WASH disruption and population movement can amplify outbreaks; verify country-level figures against WHO or Africa CDC reports.

signalTimeline.title: Chikungunya surveillance signal added
signalTimeline.description: Monitoring record added for chikungunya activity in mosquito-active regions; verify current country burden against specific PAHO or WHO updates.

signalTimeline.title: C. auris wastewater monitoring signal added
signalTimeline.description: Monitoring record added for C. auris wastewater and clinical surveillance interpretation; verify current state-level details against CDC NWSS, WastewaterSCAN, or local public health reporting.

signalTimeline.title: WHO DON601: 11 total cases; US case inconclusive; France and Spain confirmed
signalTimeline.description: WHO DON601 reports 11 total cases (8 confirmed, 2 probable, 1 inconclusive), 3 deaths (CFR 27%). New confirmed cases added from France and Spain. One U.S. passenger has inconclusive results — one positive and one negative from two separate laboratories; retesting underway. CDC states no confirmed U.S. cases. Genomic sequencing confirms near-identical sequences consistent with single zoonotic spillover and subsequent human-to-human transmission. MV Hondius departed Canary Islands May 11 bound for Netherlands with 25 crew and Dutch medical personnel aboard.

signalTimeline.title: Radboud UMC places 12 staff in precautionary quarantine
signalTimeline.description: 12 staff at Radboud UMC in Nijmegen, Netherlands, placed in precautionary quarantine after an incorrect blood-sampling procedure was followed with a confirmed Andes hantavirus patient.

signalTimeline.title: ECDC confirms 11 cases; Emory patient tests negative
signalTimeline.description: ECDC epidemiological update confirms 11 total cases (9 confirmed, 2 probable). Emory University Hospital reports mildly symptomatic U.S. passenger tests NEGATIVE for hantavirus. All passengers repatriated; MV Hondius en route Rotterdam.

signalTimeline.title: All passengers disembarked; WHO confirms 11 total cases
signalTimeline.description: All MV Hondius passengers disembarked at Tenerife. U.S. passengers (18 total) arrive Offutt AFB; 15 to UNMC quarantine, 1 confirmed positive to biocontainment, 2 to Emory Atlanta. French female confirmed positive; Spanish male preliminary positive. WHO: 11 cases (9 confirmed, 2 probable).

signalTimeline.title: MV Hondius arrives Tenerife; evacuations begin
signalTimeline.description: MV Hondius arrives Tenerife. UK passengers (22 total: 19 passengers, 3 crew) flown to Manchester; 72-hour quarantine at Arrowe Park Hospital, then 45-day home isolation. WHO Director-General Tedros present at Tenerife.

signalTimeline.title: Pathoplexus publishes multi-sequence analysis confirming single zoonotic spill
signalTimeline.description: Pathoplexus publishes analysis of sequences from 5 patients on Virological.org. Sequences are all very similar to each other, suggesting a single zoonotic spill origin.

signalTimeline.title: CDC issues HAN 528
signalTimeline.description: CDC issues Health Alert Network 528 for U.S. clinical awareness, infection prevention, and exposure reporting for Andes virus.

signalTimeline.title: CDC issues HAN 528; complete genome sequence published
signalTimeline.description: CDC issues Health Alert Network advisory 528. 8 cases (6 confirmed, 2 suspected), 3 deaths. Complete genome sequence ANDV/Switzerland/Hu-3337/2026 published on Virological.org. KLM flight attendant hospitalized Amsterdam UMC. NJ DOH announces monitoring 2 residents.

signalTimeline.title: CDC deploys team to Canary Islands; France activates emergency response
signalTimeline.description: CDC deploys team to Canary Islands to support response. ANRS France activates Level 1 Emergency Response.

signalTimeline.title: ECDC Rapid Risk Assessment published
signalTimeline.description: ECDC publishes a Rapid Risk Assessment for the cruise-ship hantavirus cluster, recommending escalation to airborne precautions during aerosol-generating procedures.

signalTimeline.title: WHO confirms Andes virus; ECDC publishes Rapid Risk Assessment
signalTimeline.description: WHO confirms Andes virus as the causative agent. ECDC publishes Rapid Risk Assessment. Ship departs Cape Verde for Tenerife. Three patients evacuated to Netherlands (Leiden UMC, Radboud UMC).

signalTimeline.title: Swiss case confirmed by Geneva University Hospitals
signalTimeline.description: Swiss case confirmed by Geneva University Hospitals — passenger who had disembarked at Saint Helena on April 24.

signalTimeline.title: WHO publishes Disease Outbreak News DON599
signalTimeline.description: WHO issues DON599 describing the initial Andes hantavirus cluster aboard MV Hondius and the multinational case-finding effort.

signalTimeline.title: WHO publishes DON599 — 7 cases, 3 deaths
signalTimeline.description: WHO publishes Disease Outbreak News 599. 7 cases: 2 confirmed, 5 suspected. 3 deaths. Identification of Andes virus performed through genomic sequencing at NICD and virus-specific PCR at Geneva University Hospitals.

signalTimeline.title: Ship docks Praia, Cape Verde; PCR confirms Case 2
signalTimeline.description: MV Hondius docks at Praia, Cape Verde. No disembarkation — local facilities insufficient for isolation. PCR posthumously confirms Case 2 had Andes virus.

signalTimeline.title: Case 3 PCR confirms hantavirus; Case 4 dies aboard
signalTimeline.description: Case 3 PCR confirms hantavirus. Case 4 (German female) dies aboard the vessel. WHO notified by UK IHR Focal Point.

signalTimeline.title: MV Hondius expedition completes voyage; index case identified
signalTimeline.description: Initial Andes hantavirus case linked to the MV Hondius expedition is identified after disembarkation; multinational case-finding begins.

signalTimeline.title: Case 4 (German female) symptom onset
signalTimeline.description: Case 4, a German female passenger, develops symptoms consistent with hantavirus pulmonary syndrome while aboard MV Hondius.

signalTimeline.title: Case 3 medically evacuated from Ascension Island
signalTimeline.description: Case 3 (British male presenting with pneumonia) medically evacuated from Ascension Island to a South Africa ICU.

signalTimeline.title: Case 2 dies at Johannesburg emergency department
signalTimeline.description: Case 2 (Dutch female) dies at a Johannesburg emergency department after deteriorating en route on the Airlink flight from Saint Helena. PCR later posthumously confirms Andes virus.

signalTimeline.title: Case 2 boards Airlink flight; briefly boards KLM KL592
signalTimeline.description: Case 2 boards Airlink flight (82 pax, 6 crew) from Saint Helena to Johannesburg, deteriorating en route. Briefly boards KLM KL592 (Johannesburg → Amsterdam) but is removed before takeoff after approximately 45 minutes. KL592 departs without her.

signalTimeline.title: Body of Case 1 removed; Case 2 disembarks Saint Helena
signalTimeline.description: Body of Case 1 (Dutch male who died April 11) removed to Saint Helena. Case 2 (Dutch female) and approximately 30 total passengers disembark at Saint Helena.

signalTimeline.title: Ship stops at Tristan da Cunha
signalTimeline.description: MV Hondius stops at Tristan da Cunha April 13–15. One island resident later suspected of exposure.

signalTimeline.title: Case 1 dies aboard MV Hondius
signalTimeline.description: Case 1 (Dutch male) dies aboard MV Hondius. No PCR performed at time of death. Symptom onset had occurred approximately April 6.

signalTimeline.title: Case 1 symptom onset
signalTimeline.description: Case 1 (Dutch male passenger) develops initial symptoms: fever, headache, and mild diarrhea while aboard MV Hondius.

signalTimeline.title: MV Hondius departs Ushuaia, Argentina
signalTimeline.description: MV Hondius departs Ushuaia, Argentina with 147 passengers and crew from 23 nationalities on an Antarctic expedition cruise.
